register_notice: Append-only. Entries are never deleted or edited in place; corrections are new entries linked via superseded_by.

entries.document_title: ASIC issues early observations on sustainability reporting ahead of 30 June 2026
entries.summary: ASIC published early observations from a desktop review of sustainability reports prepared under Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act 2001, to assist reporting entities ahead of the 30 June 2026 reporting season. Published 18 May 2026.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent title and content matching this exact ASIC news item. Publication date and full article text (the 'Published 18 May 2026' byline, the six early-observations items, and the 'next steps' section referencing 26-098MR) were then directly confirmed on 2026-07-21 from the live page text, supplied by the operator (the sandbox itself still cannot fetch asic.gov.au directly). document_hash remains unset since this was a text capture, not a raw byte fetch — the GitHub Actions runner's hash-capture pass can still complete that.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: ASIC issues early observations on sustainability reporting ahead of 30 June 2026 (259 reports lodged: 34 listed, 225 unlisted)
entries.summary: Per ASIC's early observations release (published 18 May 2026), 259 sustainability reports were lodged for the financial year ending 31 December 2025 (34 from listed entities, 225 from unlisted entities), reviewed for compliance with the Corporations Act and AASB S2.
entries.verification_notes: Same source document as E-001. Source URL and article text cross-confirmed via independent search queries and, on 2026-07-21, a direct text capture of the live page supplied by the operator confirming the article itself, its date, and 'Figure 1 sets out an overview of the total number of sustainability reports lodged with ASIC as of 6 May 2026'. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the specific 259/34/225 figures live in that Figure 1 image, not in the plain text captured — the direct capture confirms the figure exists and its data-as-of date (6 May 2026), but does not itself independently confirm the exact numbers, which still rest on the earlier search-engine corroboration only. Treat those specific numbers as MEDIUM rather than fully document-confirmed until the image or its 'text version' link is captured directly.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL and specific figures independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: ASIC's administration of the sustainability reporting requirements
entries.summary: ASIC's stated approach to administering the Chapter 2M sustainability reporting regime: a 'pragmatic and proportionate' approach to supervision and enforcement during the transition period, including engaging with reporting entities where disclosures appear incorrect or misleading, and being more likely to commence an enforcement investigation where there is misconduct of a serious or reckless nature, or where a reporting entity fails to prepare a required sustainability report at all. Also describes ASIC's discretion to grant relief from the reporting/audit requirements where compliance would make a report misleading, be inappropriate, or impose unreasonable burdens.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent title, URL, and substantive content (pragmatic/proportionate approach, enforcement triggers, relief discretion) matching this exact ASIC resource page. This is an evergreen resource page with no publication byline. document_hash remains unset since this sandbox cannot fetch asic.gov.au directly; the GitHub Actions runner's hash-capture pass can complete that.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: Sustainability Assurance implementation support hub (ASSA 5000 / ASSA 5010)
entries.summary: The Auditing and Assurance Standards Board's implementation support hub for the sustainability assurance framework underpinning Chapter 2M sustainability reports: ASSA 5000 (General Requirements for Sustainability Assurance Engagements, approved 28 January 2025, applying to reporting periods beginning on or after 1 January 2025) and ASSA 5010 (the timeline specifying the phase-in of limited and reasonable assurance for Group 1, 2 and 3 reporting entities from 1 January 2025 to 30 June 2030).
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent content (ASSA 5000/5010 titles, approval date, phase-in timeline) matching this exact AUASB hub page, corroborated further by a third independent search. This is an evergreen resource page with no publication byline. document_hash remains unset since this sandbox cannot fetch auasb.gov.au directly; the GitHub Actions runner's hash-capture pass can complete that. Directly on-topic: AUASB is the standard-setter for the mandatory assurance requirements that attach to Chapter 2M sustainability reports, the audit/assurance side of the same regime already covered by E-006/E-007 (ANAO) and E-014 (ASIC's own audit FAQs).

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: ASIC updates financial reporting relief instruments
entries.summary: ASIC remade two legislative instruments that directly affect Chapter 2M sustainability report administration: ASIC Corporations (Electronic Lodgment of Financial and Sustainability Reports) Instrument 2026/59 (replacing the 2016/181 instrument, extending electronic-lodgment relief for exchange-listed entities' financial, sustainability and directors' reports to Cboe Australia-listed entities, expiring 1 April 2031), and ASIC Corporations (Amendment) Instrument 2026/313, which amends the Related Schemes Reports Instrument 2025/438 to let a registered scheme include its related schemes' sustainability disclosures within a single combined sustainability report — something the Corporations Act sustainability reporting requirements did not previously expressly permit.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent instrument numbers, titles, and substantive content matching this exact ASIC news item. document_hash remains unset since this sandbox cannot fetch asic.gov.au directly; the GitHub Actions runner's hash-capture pass can complete that. Directly on-topic: both instruments amend the operational/lodgment mechanics of the Chapter 2M sustainability reporting regime this case tracks.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: Climate related financial reporting and guidance (Treasury.gov.au)
entries.summary: Treasury's policy page for the mandatory climate-related financial disclosure regime, tracking the May 2026 Federal Budget proposal to raise the 'Group 3' proprietary-company reporting thresholds from $50m to $100m consolidated revenue and $25m to $50m consolidated gross assets (the 100-employee threshold unchanged), which would move some private companies out of scope entirely, plus a flagged Treasury consultation on three narrower settings: clarifying how 'undue cost or effort' applies, recalibrating assurance settings to be proportionate to reporter size/maturity, and setting clearer boundaries on supplier information requests. As of the most recent reporting these measures are proposed only, subject to consultation and legislative design, not yet enacted.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located via search and cross-confirmed via multiple independent legal/accounting-firm summaries (BDO, Allens, CA ANZ, Accounting Times) describing consistent specific figures (the $50m->$100m and $25m->$50m threshold changes, the three consultation areas) all attributed to the same May 2026 Budget measure. Confidence set to MEDIUM rather than HIGH because the sandbox could not directly view the live Treasury page text itself (only search-result snippets and secondary summaries converged on it) and because the measure is explicitly described as proposed/not yet legislated. document_hash unset for the same reason; the GitHub Actions runner's hash-capture pass can complete a direct fetch. Directly on-topic: this is a live proposed change to the scope of the Chapter 2M regime this case tracks.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL corroborated via multiple independent secondary summaries rather than a direct view of the primary page text; document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch.

entries.document_title: Sustainability Assurance - FAQs
entries.summary: AUASB FAQs supplementing ASSA 5000, addressing practical assurance questions for Chapter 2M sustainability reports: the difference between AASB S2's whole-of-GPFR materiality lens and ASSA 5000's assurance-scope-only lens; that later-year voluntary/non-assured sustainability disclosures (e.g. in the Operating and Financial Review, or voluntary AASB S1/climate disclosures) fall outside assurance scope; the 'another practitioner' provisions for entities using a different firm for NGERS Scope 1/2 emissions assurance than for the Act sustainability report; and that entities may appoint one auditor or separate auditors for their financial and sustainability reports, except RSEs which must use the same auditor for both.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via search results returning consistent, specific FAQ content (materiality distinction, NGERS 'another practitioner' provisions, auditor appointment options) matching this exact AUASB page, corroborated by a related ASIC news item on the same FAQ topic. document_hash remains unset since this sandbox cannot fetch auasb.gov.au directly. Directly on-topic: this is a distinct sub-page of the AUASB assurance hub already partly captured as E-019, addressing the practical assurance mechanics of the same Chapter 2M regime.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: ASIC launches e-learning educational modules on sustainability reporting
entries.summary: ASIC, in partnership with the AASB, released all eight sustainability-reporting educational modules in interactive e-learning format in May 2026, aimed at Group 2 and 3 entities not yet subject to the reporting requirements (commencing financial years on or after 1 July 2026 and 1 July 2027 respectively). entries.document_title: Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Reporting System Reform) Bill 2026
entries.summary: The Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Reporting System Reform) Bill 2026 merges the Financial Reporting Council, the AASB and the AUASB — the two standard-setting bodies already tracked in this case (E-006/E-007/E-019/E-022/E-024 relate to their sustainability-assurance and audit functions) — into a single new body, External Reporting Australia, with an overarching Governing Council and three standard-setting boards covering accounting, audit and assurance, and sustainability reporting. Multiple independent sources report the bill has passed Parliament, with Greens-secured amendments barring representatives of large auditing/consulting firms from sitting on the new Governing Council or its standard-setting boards. Commencement of parts of Schedule 1 is tied to Royal Assent (transitional provisions from the later of 30 June 2026 or the day after Royal Assent; substantive provisions from the later of 1 November 2026 or four months after Royal Assent).
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent detail (the merger structure, the External Reporting Australia name, the Greens amendment, the commencement dates) matching this exact Parliament of Australia bill page, corroborated by the bill's Austlii explanatory memorandum and independent accounting-industry press. document_hash remains unset since this sandbox cannot fetch aph.gov.au directly. Directly on-topic: a major institutional restructure of the bodies that set and administer the sustainability-assurance standards for the Chapter 2M regime this case tracks.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Reporting System Reform) Act 2026 (Act No. 61, 2026, C2026A00061)
entries.summary: The Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Reporting System Reform) Bill 2026 received Royal Assent and was registered on the Federal Register of Legislation on 8 July 2026 as Act No. 61, 2026 (C2026A00061). The Act merges the FRC, AASB and AUASB into a new body, External Reporting Australia, responsible for accounting, auditing and assurance, and sustainability standards — confirming and finalising the reform already tracked as E-025 (bill-stage material).
entries.verification_notes: The specific identifier (Act No. 61, 2026; C2026A00061; registered 8 July 2026) was independently returned and cross-confirmed across three separate search queries in this pass, with consistent detail each time. entries.document_title: ASIC and AASB team up to help smaller companies get ready for sustainability reporting
entries.summary: ASIC and the AASB hosted a series of free in-person workshops in May 2026 across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane and Perth, delivered by the University of Technology Sydney, for smaller and mid-size companies beginning their preparation for mandatory sustainability reporting (financial years commencing on or after 1 July 2026/2027). The workshops featured expert-led presentations, discussions and practical activities.

entries.document_title: AASB S2025-1 Amendments to Greenhouse Gas Emissions Disclosures
entries.summary: The AASB issued AASB S2025-1 on 17 December 2025, amending AASB S2 Climate-related Disclosures to provide additional relief and clarify existing relief for Scope 3 Category 15 (financed emissions) greenhouse gas emissions disclosures, including permitting entities to limit measurement/disclosure to financed emissions and permitting classification systems other than GICS to disaggregate financed emissions data. The amendments align with equivalent ISSB amendments to IFRS S2 (issued 11 December 2025) and apply to annual reporting periods beginning on or after 1 January 2027.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent detail (issue date, Scope 3 Category 15 relief content, ISSB alignment, 2027 application date) matching this exact AASB standard document, corroborated by a Grant Thornton sustainability reporting alert and the AASB's own S2 amendments FAQ page. document_hash remains unset since this sandbox cannot fetch aasb.gov.au directly. Directly on-topic: a substantive amendment to AASB S2, the core standard underpinning the Chapter 2M regime this case tracks.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: Parliamentary Joint Committee on Corporations and Financial Services, Opening Statement, 29 May 2026
entries.summary: Then-ASIC Chair Joe Longo's opening statement to the Parliamentary Joint Committee on Corporations and Financial Services inquiry into oversight of ASIC, delivered in his final appearance before the Committee after five years as Chair (accompanied by incoming Chair Sarah Court and other Commissioners). Addresses ASIC's financial reporting, sustainability reporting and audit priorities, describing a 'pragmatic and proportionate' early-years supervision and enforcement approach to mandatory Chapter 2M sustainability/climate reporting, ongoing surveillance of Group 1 entity sustainability reports, and engagement with large audit firms on assurance methodologies.
entries.verification_notes: Confirmed via two independent WebSearch queries returning identical title, date and URL; sandbox cannot directly fetch asic.gov.au (blocked), so verification rests on cross-referenced search corroboration per Factualism Audit rules for this environment.
